ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: During the past 6 months, how many times have you and your sex partner discussed how to prevent pregnancy?
Notes: 0=Never; 1=Sometimes (1-3 times); 2=Often (4-6 times); 3=A lot (7 or more times); -999=Missing/Unknown

ElementDescription: During the past 6 months, how many times have you and your sex partner discussed how to use condoms?
Notes: 0=Never; 1=Sometimes (1-3 times); 2=Often (4-6 times); 3=A lot (7 or more times); -999=Missing/Unknown

ElementDescription: During the past 6 months, how many times have you and your sex partner discussed how to prevent the AIDS virus?
Notes: 0=Never; 1=Sometimes (1-3 times); 2=Often (4-6 times); 3=A lot (7 or more times); -999=Missing/Unknown

ElementDescription: During the past 6 months, how many times have you and your sex partner discussed how to prevent STDs?
Notes: 0=Never; 1=Sometimes (1-3 times); 2=Often (4-6 times); 3=A lot (7 or more times); -999=Missing/Unknown

ElementDescription: During the past 6 months, how many times have you and your sex partner discussed your partner's sex history?
Notes: 0=Never; 1=Sometimes (1-3 times); 2=Often (4-6 times); 3=A lot (7 or more times); -999=Missing/Unknown
